seo.title: Docker & Containers implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Docker & Containers projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides actionable verification steps for securing, optimizing, and deploying Docker containers to production environments. It focuses on reducing attack surfaces, ensuring resource availability, and maintaining operational visibility.

items.title: Implement Non-Root User
items.description: Verify the Dockerfile includes a 'USER' instruction with a specific UID/GID to prevent the container from running as root.

items.title: Pin Base Image Versions
items.description: Replace 'latest' tags with specific version numbers or SHA256 digests to ensure build reproducibility and prevent breaking changes.

items.title: Utilize Multi-Stage Builds
items.description: Separate the build environment from the runtime environment to ensure the production image contains only the compiled binary and necessary dependencies.

items.title: Clean Package Manager Caches
items.description: Ensure 'apt-get clean' or equivalent commands are executed within the same 'RUN' layer as package installation to minimize image size.

items.title: Scan for Vulnerabilities
items.description: Run an automated scan using Trivy or Docker Scout and verify that no 'Critical' or 'High' severity CVEs exist in the final image.

items.title: Define Memory Limits
items.description: Set hard memory limits (e.g., 'mem_limit' in Compose) to prevent a single container from causing host-wide Out-Of-Memory (OOM) events.

items.title: Set CPU Reservations
items.description: Configure CPU limits or shares to ensure the host scheduler provides enough cycles for critical system processes and other containers.

items.title: Configure Restart Policies
items.description: Set the restart policy to 'unless-stopped' or 'on-failure' to ensure service availability after crashes or host reboots.

items.title: Enable Log Rotation
items.description: Configure the 'json-file' or 'journald' log driver with 'max-size' and 'max-file' options to prevent disk exhaustion from container logs.

items.title: Set Ulimits
items.description: Adjust 'nofile' and 'nproc' ulimits in the container configuration to handle the expected number of concurrent connections or processes.

items.title: Use Custom Bridge Networks
items.description: Verify that containers are attached to user-defined bridge networks rather than the default bridge to enable automatic DNS resolution and isolation.

items.title: Restrict Port Exposure
items.description: Audit the 'EXPOSE' instruction and '-p' flags to ensure only the minimum required ports are accessible to the host or external network.

items.title: Apply Read-Only Root Filesystem
items.description: Run the container with the '--read-only' flag and use temporary volumes for directories requiring write access (e.g., /tmp).

items.title: Disable Inter-Container Communication
items.description: Where isolation is required, set '--icc=false' on the Docker daemon or use network aliases to strictly control traffic between services.

items.title: Enable No-New-Privileges
items.description: Set the 'no-new-privileges' security option to prevent processes from gaining new privileges via setuid or setgid binaries.

items.title: Use Named Volumes for Persistence
items.description: Ensure all stateful data is stored in named Docker volumes rather than the container's writable layer to prevent data loss during container recreation.

items.title: Avoid Bind Mounts in Production
items.description: Replace host bind mounts with named volumes or specialized drivers to reduce dependency on specific host filesystem paths.

items.title: Verify Volume Backup Strategy
items.description: Test the restoration process of volume data from snapshots or off-site backups to ensure business continuity.

items.title: Clean Up Unused Volumes
items.description: Implement a routine 'docker volume prune' or manual audit to reclaim disk space from orphaned volumes.

items.title: Set Volume Mount Permissions
items.description: Explicitly set the ':ro' (read-only) flag on volume mounts that the container should not have permission to modify.

items.title: Define Healthchecks
items.description: Include a 'HEALTHCHECK' instruction that verifies the application is actually serving requests, not just that the process is running.

items.title: Handle SIGTERM Gracefully
items.description: Verify the application responds to SIGTERM by closing database connections and finishing active requests within the stop grace period.

items.title: Externalize Secrets
items.description: Confirm no secrets (API keys, passwords) are stored in environment variables visible to 'docker inspect'; use Docker Secrets or a Vault provider.

items.title: Standardize Logging
items.description: Ensure the application logs exclusively to stdout and stderr to allow the Docker daemon to capture and forward logs to a central aggregator.

items.title: Use .dockerignore
items.description: Verify a .dockerignore file exists to prevent sensitive local files (.env, .git, SSH keys) from being copied into the image build context.
